audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed as Job's reply: longing to find God's seat, legal confidence, hiddenness in every direction, holding the divine path, and trembling before Shaddai's decree.

verses.restored: Even today my complaint is rebellion; my hand is heavy because of my groaning.
If only I knew where I could find him, that I might come to his seat!
I would arrange my case before him and fill my mouth with arguments.
I would know the words he would answer me and understand what he would say to me.
Would he contend with me in great power? No; he himself would pay attention to me.
There an upright person could argue with him, and I would be delivered forever from my judge.
Look, I go east, but he is not there; west, but I do not perceive him;
north, where he works, but I do not behold him; south, but I do not see him.
But he knows the way with me; when he has tested me, I shall come out like gold.
My foot has held fast to his steps; I have kept his way and not turned aside.
From the commandment of his lips I have not departed; I have treasured the words of his mouth more than my portion.
But he is one, and who can turn him? What his soul desires, he does.
For he completes what is appointed for me, and many such things are with him.
Therefore I am terrified before his face; I consider, and I dread him.
The Divine has made my heart soft, and Shaddai has terrified me.
Yet I am not cut off before darkness, nor has he covered deep gloom from my face.
